description: This is a false-colour satellite image depicting the CORINE Land Cover (CLC) class 6.1.2, "Sparse vegetation on rocks", located in Agalar, Anatolia, Turkey. The image was acquired on 2011-06-29 using SPOT-5 data with a 2.5 m resolution, presented as a Near Infrared (NIR)/Red (R)/Green (G) band combination. The image displays a mountainous or rugged landscape. Dominant land cover features include extensive areas of reddish-brown and pink tones, intermixed with bright turquoise, representing various forms of sparsely vegetated ground and exposed rock. Darker, greenish-black regions are visible, particularly in the upper and middle parts of the image, indicating shaded slopes or deep valleys. Small, scattered light blue patches are also present across the lower sections.
